executive_summary: Global markets experience significant relief as geopolitical tensions ease following the end of the Ukraine-Russia conflict, driving stocks to new highs while the dollar and fear indicators unwind sharply. Federal Reserve regulatory actions and SEC enforcement leadership changes signal ongoing oversight of financial institutions.

key_insights.insight: War is over for Wall Street: stocks, dollar and fear index unwind moves
Nasdaq sets record highs as investors return to tech stocks
SEC appoints David Woodcock as Director of Division of Enforcement, signaling heightened regulatory oversight
Fed terminates enforcement actions with Crédit Agricole, Mega International Commercial Bank, and Goldman Sachs
SEC approves customer cross-margining in U.S. Treasury market, changing margin requirements
Higher oil prices and yields fail to dampen US stocks despite pause in rate cuts
FOMC minutes March 17-18, 2026 reveal Fed policy stance
Discount rate meeting minutes February 9 and March 18, 2026
Pakistan seeks guarantees for second US-Iran talks amid Strait of Hormuz concerns
April Fed meeting expectations show no imminent rate cuts

blind_spot: Federal Reserve discount rate meeting discussions reveal internal debate on monetary policy direction that may not be fully priced in by markets
